note: Greek tokens per pure-asemic-low-T vox (n=49), measured directly with each model's tokenizer (no weights). Tests the byte-fragmentation mechanism for the deep-Greek effect.
result: Monotonic: more Greek fragmentation -> stronger deep-Greek name-adjacency. Precision (4-bit Gemma+Mistral at opposite ends) and size (3B/7B/9B) do not explain it. Direct measure, not vocab-size proxy.

matched_factorial_mistral.note: v2 falsifier (voces_falsifier-v2-lexicality_Mistral-7B-v0.3_results.json): holds Greek-token fragmentation fixed at ~11 and varies one axis at a time; bootstrap 95% CIs; all four cohorts one model + one pair of centroids (magnitudes comparable). Resolves what the v1 nonname_falsifier (confounded, ~2x frag mismatch) could not. Replicates v1: voces +0.040 vs +0.041; low-frag -0.005.

LEXICALITY.reading: meaninglessness drives it (co-equal); meaningful Greek does NOT persist

NAMEHOOD.reading: no namehood -- voces persist deep LESS than matched random asemic; reversed

matched_factorial_mistral.mechanism: the deep region holds Greek that is BOTH heavily fragmented AND meaning-less (fragmented nonsense); neither alone suffices; the voces sit just below pure noise because they are a recognizable genre (H1)

caveats: n=3 models for the cross-model ordering, monotone-by-chance prob 1/3; co-varies with family+vocab+training Greek fraction (correlational)
the matched factorial is SINGLE-MODEL (Mistral); non-name arms n=28-29; a 2nd model owed
'meaningful Greek' = long Greek loanforms, may carry morphemic (not just byte) tokens
gemma_tokenizer_source: unsloth/gemma-2-9b (non-gated mirror, identical SentencePiece tokenizer to google/gemma-2-9b)
